message:
fix(rwlock): extend pre-grant fairness to OwnedRead/OwnedWrite futures and add explicit state drops

Apply the same pre-grant lock ownership pattern from ReadFuture/WriteFuture
to the Owned variants:

OwnedReadFuture::poll:
- Check waiter_id first: if dequeued, state.readers was pre-incremented
  by release_writer, return Ready with OwnedRwLockReadGuard
- If still in queue, update waker and Pending
- Only try fast-path for new arrivals (no waiter_id)

OwnedWriteFuture::poll:
- Check waiter_id first: if dequeued, writer_active was pre-set by
  release_writer, decrement writer_waiters and return Ready
- If still in queue, update waker and Pending
- Only try fast-path for new arrivals with writer_waiters==1

Also adds explicit drop(state) calls in release_writer before returning
the (waker, reader_wakers) tuple, ensuring the mutex guard is released
before waking any waiters.

Flattens if/else blocks in ReadFuture and WriteFuture for consistency
(dequeued branch no longer nested inside else).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
